business.headline: HDFC Bank shares fall over 2% after report on Rs 45 crore payment issueThe stock dropped nearly 2.6% during the trading session after the report surfaced. As per NSE data cited in the report, HDFC Bank shares were trading at Rs 758.75, down Rs 20.15 or 2.59%, around 1:29 pm.

8th Pay Commission: Why a union wants 5 different fitment factors instead of oneThe Indian Railway Technical Supervisors' Association (IRTSA) has urged the 8th Pay Commission to replace a single fitment factor with five graded multipliers. The demand shifts the discussion from overall salary hikes to how different levels should be paid.

Air India trims domestic flights for July-August as fuel costs soarIn a statement, an Air India spokesperson said the airline has temporarily adjusted some domestic services after earlier changes to international routes.

How AI helped Taiwan overtake India to become the world's 5th largest stock marketTaiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is the world's largest contract chipmaker and plays a major role in producing advanced semiconductors used in AI systems.

Not a finding of fraud, dishonesty: Byju Raveendran on jail sentence in SingaporeResponding to the developments, Raveendran said the matter has been misunderstood and is part of ongoing settlement talks with lenders and stakeholders.

From Messi to mounting lawsuits: Inside the collapse of Byju's empireA Singapore court sentenced Byju Raveendran to six months in jail for contempt over asset-related orders. The ruling sharpens focus on Byju's steep fall from a $22 billion edtech darling to a company facing debt, lawsuits and insolvency proceedings.

Air India, BigBasket, Tata Electronics: Inside Tata's key boardroom discussionsTata Sons' latest board meeting stayed calm as operating chiefs answered questions on Air India, BigBasket and other businesses. The discussions showed tighter scrutiny of profits, capital allocation and the group's long-term strategic bets.

Sensex, Nifty open flat as Middle East tensions keep investors cautiousThe S&P BSE Sensex slipped 22.10 points, or 0.03%, to 75,987.60 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 6.95 points, or 0.03%, to 23,906.75.

Byju Raveendran sentenced to six months in jail by Singapore courtByju Raveendran, founder of embattled edtech company Byju's, has been sentenced to six months in jail by a Singapore court for contempt of court.

8th Pay Commission: Odisha consultations scheduled for July 6-7The 8th Pay Commission will visit Bhubaneswar on July 6 and 7 for consultations with organisations, unions and employee associations. The outreach comes as the panel widens regional talks and gathers demands on pay, allowances and pensions.

Indian economy is quite robust: Arvind PanagariyaIn an exclusive interview with India Today, Arvind Panagariya, Chairman of the sixteenth Finance Commission, asserts that the domestic economy remains fundamentally robust, citing a steady gross foreign direct investment of ninety-four billion dollars for the recent fiscal year and consistent growth averaging over seven percent in the past three years.

When centralised manufacturing, record irregularities conveniently escaped detectionIn the eighth part of this series on labour laws and Inspector Raj, this article examines how seemingly separate establishments functioned as one operational unit, while material irregularities in records and manufacturing activity went unnoticed.

Hidden cost of AI adoption: Why enterprises are worried about token economicsEnterprises are reassessing AI roll-outs as token and compute costs climb with wider internal use. The shift is pushing companies to focus on cost control, ROI and sustainable scale.

When it rains, it pours: Why Nithin Kamath thinks 2026 could be tough for householdsIn a post on X, Kamath flagged concerns around a possible super El Nino, weak rainfall and rising energy costs linked to global tensions. According to him, if food and fuel prices rise together, inflation could become a much bigger challenge for Indian households.

ITR filing this year? 5 changes that make it different from last yearPlanning to file your ITR soon? Before you begin, it may be worth slowing down for a moment. From new disclosure requirements to tweaks in reporting rules, tax return filing this year comes with a few important changes that many taxpayers may easily miss. Here are five key updates to know.

Markets end lower as renewed Iran tensions push rupee downThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 479.26 points, or 0.63%, to close at 76,009.70, while the NSE Nifty50 declined 118 points, or 0.49%, to settle at 23,913.70.

Rs 24 lakh for 1 training session from AI gurus? Wall Street says it's worth itFormer SoftBank investors Felipe Sinisterra and Dave Wang are charging banks nearly Rs 24 lakh for one-day AI workshops. The rush for these sessions reflects how AI is becoming essential to productivity, hiring and career survival in finance.

21–30 year olds drive 46% of education loans: Why are young Indians borrowing?Are young Indians borrowing only for emergencies and shopping anymore? Not quite. A growing number are now taking loans for something very different, i.e., learning new skills and improving career prospects. Let's have a look.

What if 8th Pay Commission gets delayed? Here's the impact on salary, HRA and arrearsThe 8th Pay Commission is moving through consultations, but many employees have one key question i.e., what if implementation gets delayed? The answer could have implications for salary, HRA and arrears.
Rupee is falling but is it alarming?The rupee's recent slide has sparked concern, but is it truly a sign of economic stress or just a reflection of global forces at play?

entertainment.headline: Imtiaz Ali has his 'eyes on' Alia Bhatt, hints at reunion after Highway Imtiaz Ali has hinted at working with Alia Bhatt again more than a decade after Highway. The remark has revived interest in the film that reshaped her career and remains a key title in his body of work.

Governor trailer: Manoj Bajpayee is RBI chief during India's 1990 financial crisis The trailer of Governor shows Manoj Bajpayee as an RBI chief navigating India's 1990 financial crisis amid rising public unrest and political pressure. The film also stars Adah Sharma as a journalist questioning the controversial decisions taken during the turbulent period.

Obsession review: Curry Barker's psychological horror is impossible to shake offRelease Date:Fri May 29, 2026 Curry Barker's Obsession follows a lonely young man whose wish for reciprocated love turns catastrophic. The film uses that premise to explore loneliness, entitlement and the horror of treating love like possession.

Sri Sri Ravi Shankar meets celebs: Vikrant Massey touches feet, Sunil Grover jokes Actors Vikrant Massey, Sunil Grover, director Rajkumar Hirani and producer Sajid Nadiadwala, among others, recently met spiritual leader Sri Sri Ravi Shankar. Several videos and photos from the interaction have now surfaced online.

Ranveer Singh seeks 'purpose of life' amid Don 3 row, meets Sadhguru for answers Ranveer Singh met Sadhguru and asked him about the purpose of life in a video shared online. The interaction surfaced as FWICE issued a non-cooperation directive and Excel Entertainment sought Rs 45 crore in the Don 3 row.

Vishal Dadlani denies Indian Idol exit after NEET paper leak criticism Vishal Dadlani dismissed rumours that he had been removed from Indian Idol after his NEET remarks. His post shifted attention to online misinformation and his criticism of the exam leak response.

Zoya Akhtar reacts to theft at Tiger Baby Films, confirms it was an inside job Zoya Akhtar reacted after a major theft was reported at Tiger Baby Digital LLP, where several hard disks containing sensitive production data were allegedly stolen and sold. The filmmaker called it an inside job and revealed that the company has backup copies of all the missing files.

Inside Karan Johar's birthday party: Kajol, Kareena, Alia-Ranbir glam up the night Designer Manish Malhotra shared inside pictures from filmmaker Karan Johar's birthday party, revealing a guest list packed with film stars. The celebrities that attended the bash were Kajol, Kareena and Karisma Kapoor, Vicky Kaushal, Kiara Advani and others.

Taylor Swift sends guitar, note to 8-year-old fan after viral video Taylor Swift sent a guitar and handwritten note to an eight-year-old fan in Ohio after her paper-airplane song request to a neighbour went viral.

Virat Kohli rushes to hug Anushka Sharma after RCB enters IPL 2026 final. Watch Actor Anushka Sharma and cricketer Virat Kohli left fans emotional after sharing a heartfelt hug following RCB's victory over GT in IPL 2026 Qualifier 1. Videos of Anushka cheering, praying and celebrating with Virat quickly went viral across social media.

FWICE, CINTAA and more: Don 3 row puts Bollywood's film bodies in focus The Don 3 fallout between Ranveer Singh and Farhan Akhtar has turned attention to Bollywood's film bodies. The episode has highlighted how unions, guilds and trade bodies shape work, disputes and releases across the industry.

Before Ranveer Singh, FWICE banned Diljit Dosanjh, Mika, Gauahar Khan. Here's why Ranveer Singh isn't the first artist to face a FWICE boycott. The film body has initiated similar action against others lately, and reasons range from Pakistan to Covid-19. Read to know who was banned for what.

Every masterpiece has cheap copy: Internet roasts Varun's Chunnari Chunnari remake The recreated Chunnari Chunnari from Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai has been unveiled with Varun Dhawan, Pooja Hegde and Mrunal Thakur. Its release has triggered online criticism and nostalgic comparisons with Biwi No. 1's track featuring Salman Khan and Sushmita Sen.

Bollywood replaces actors every Friday. So why punish Ranveer for leaving a film? Bollywood replaces actors overnight and calls it "creative differences." But when a star walks away on his own terms, suddenly it becomes a matter of "discipline."

Shobhaa De backs Ananya Panday amid Bharatanatyam row over Chand Mera Dil Novelist and columnist Shobhaa De defended Ananya Panday after her Bharatanatyam fusion dance sequence in Chand Mera Dil was trolled online. She said the scrutiny should also fall on the film's choreographer and director, not the actor alone.

Varun can't become Salman Khan: Singer Abhijeet on Chunnari Chunnari remake row Abhijeet Bhattacharya has criticised the recreated Chunnari Chunnari version in Varun Dhawan's Hai Jawani Toh Ishq Hona Hai. He said the remake diluted a long-running hit.

The Burj Khalifa dream: What it really costs, celebs who own luxury homes there The Burj Khalifa houses some of the world's most expensive apartments, with prices ranging from a few crores to over Rs 200 crore. Over the years, several Bollywood stars, global celebrities and business elites have reportedly bought homes inside Dubai's most iconic address.

Ravi Kishan files complaint over defamatory remarks damaging his reputation Actor-politician Ravi Kishan has filed a complaint alleging false and defamatory statements were spread against him across Gorakhpur damaging his image. The complaint seeks an impartial investigation and legal action against those involved.

Item songs in 2026? Ram Charan's Peddi and special tracks that don't feel special Ram Charan's Peddi has released Hellallallo with Shruti Haasan and Janhvi Kapoor as a big promotional spectacle. The backlash to the song has renewed questions over repetitive choreography, the male gaze and the fading pull of special numbers.

general news.headline: Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah to resign tomorrow: SourcesFuelling the speculation further, sources in the Chief Minister's Office confirmed that Siddaramaiah has sought an appointment with Karnataka Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot for May 28.

Can deletion during SIR cost you citizenship? Supreme Court's big orderThe Supreme Court verdict is crucial as the petitioners argued that the SIR exercise, which is being conducted by the Election Commission, was a citizenship verification exercise by backdoor.

BJP MP claims 50 Trinamool MLAs, 20 MPs ready to defect as Bengal turmoil deepensBJP MP Saumitra Khan claimed in Bankura that several Trinamool legislators and MPs were ready to join his party. The remarks, along with his attack on Abhishek Banerjee, drew a sharp denial from Trinamool and deepened speculation over unrest in the party.

SIR had become a fait accompli: Yogendra Yadav on why he skipped court hearingYogendra Yadav alleged that the SIR exercise became a "fait accompli" as the Election Commission of India continued rolling out successive phases during the Supreme Court hearing. He further said that, in his view, this effectively amounted to large-scale disenfranchisement of voters and marked a potential erosion of constitutional safeguards.

Live: Siddaramaiah seeks Governor meet tomorrow as exit buzz growsKarnataka Leadership Buzz Live Updates: Karnataka is headed for a major political transition, with sources indicating Chief Minister Siddaramaiah is likely to resign on Thursday at 11 am following marathon meetings with the Congress high command in Delhi. Siddaramaiah has also sought an appointment with Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot tomorrow, further fuelling speculation over an imminent change of guard in the state. Deputy CM D. K. Shivakumar, widely seen as the frontrunner for the top post, has extended his stay in Delhi and is expected to attend a breakfast meeting called by Siddaramaiah at the CM's Bengaluru residence on Thursday morning. Sources said crucial discussions involving Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge, senior leader Rahul Gandhi and Karnataka leaders centred around honouring the reported power-sharing understanding in the state. Even as the Congress publicly maintained that talks focused on Rajya Sabha and Legislative Council elections, the political buzz around Karnataka's leadership transition has sharply intensified. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

SIR won't decide citizenship. But how will deleted voters prove nationality?The Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission's Special Intensive Revision but said it is not a citizenship test. The ruling now shifts attention to how the Home Ministry will verify documents of deleted voters.

Head shaved, hands cuffed: Bengal cops parade arrested TMC goons in undergarmentsThe police in West Bengal have paraded at least three TMC-linked strongmen in undergarments through the streets after their arrests. Akash Singh, a don from Howrah, had his head and face shaven too. The parades come amid a police crackdown and amid the TMC's allegations of legal and moral breach.

The golf club has faced questions over racism, public access, golfers' practice rights and an ASI-protected monument.

CBSE's holy mess: How India's biggest school board lost controlWhat began as CBSE's big digital overhaul has snowballed into a full-blown credibility crisis. From crashed portals and swapped answer sheets to hacking claims and millions of students demanding scanned copies, India's biggest school board now finds itself battling one question. Can students trust the system that decides their future?

Microsoft and others rethink AI use, cost rising fast and no productivity gainsHandcrafted ByOur Editors The big promise around AI was simple: it would save companies money while making employees far more productive. But over the last two years, as AI has gone from an experiment to an everyday workplace tool, companies are getting big bills. Microsoft is reportedly pulling back on Claude because of rising costs, while Uber has already burned through its AI budget for 2026.

RFK Jr proudly handles snakes with bare hands, ignores wife's warningsThe clip, shared by RFK Jr on X, shows him handling two black racer snakes at a beachfront property while those around him watched with concern.

Live: Opposition narrative in Bihar and Bengal has collapsed, BJP on SIR verdictSC Verdict Today Live Updates: The Supreme Court has upheld Election Commission's power to conduct Special Intensive Revision (SIR) of electoral rolls. "We are equally satisfied that the object sought to be achieved by the SIR bears a direct nexus to the constitutional goal of free and fair elections," the court said in its verdict. The judgement was made on batch of petitions challenging the legality of the Election Commission's SIR exercise, which was conducted in Bihar in 2025 and earlier this year in West Bengal. The petitions had argued that the poll body lacks the statutory and constitutional authority to carry out an extensive exercise like SIR in the manner adopted in Bihar, raising concerns over voter exclusion and the scope of the Election Commission's mandate. Stay tuned to indiatoday.in for all the latest updates.

HDFC Bank shares drop 2% on report of probe into Rs 45-crore interest paymentsThe stock dropped nearly 2.6% during the trading session after the report surfaced. As per NSE data cited in the report, HDFC Bank shares were trading at Rs 758.75, down Rs 20.15 or 2.59%, around 1:29 pm.

Erin Brockovich, person and not movie, is back and she is going after AI data centresErin Brockovich has launched a public platform mapping major AI data centres and community complaints across the United States. It adds local worries over water, power, noise and planning to the wider fight over the AI infrastructure boom.

India has more cities than it knows of. Why are they hiding in plain sight?India might have far more cities and towns than the government's numbers acknowledge. An EAC-PM paper argues that millions live in human settlements that function like towns and cities but officially remain classified as rural. This adversely impacts the lives of millions of residents.

Pakistani extremists prevent Lahore from returning to original Hindu, Sikh namesWeeks after clearing the proposal, the Maryam Nawaz-led provincial government of Pakistan's Punjab has deferred the decision to restore the pre-Partition Hindu and British-era names of several Lahore landmarks. According to reports, the government was forced to make a U-turn after it faced criticism from "extremists" and social media vloggers.

Did controversial Coempt Eduteck trigger CBSE's OSM chaos? Cyber expert commentsThe controversy around CBSE's On-Screen Marking system is no longer limited to technical glitches. Questions are now being raised about the private company allegedly linked to the platform, cybersecurity warnings that surfaced months ago, and whether there could be a conflict of interest in the ongoing review of the system.

Delhi woman calls technicians to fix AC, what they do next leaves her emotionalDuring an AC service visit in Delhi, two technicians quietly paused to admire a woman's artwork at home. The simple exchange gave viewers as a reminder of how creativity can create unexpected human connections.

sports.headline: Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist, dies at 79 Indian sport mourns Randhir Singh, India's first Asian Games shooting gold medallist and veteran sports administrator, whose legacy spanned five Olympics and the global Olympic movement.

Amongst other big names, Iga Swiatek and Alexander Zverev will also be in action today.

SRH have Plan A, B and C ready for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi: Pat Cummins IPL 2026: Sunrisers Hyderabad captain Pat Cummins admitted that SRH have prepared multiple bowling plans for Rajasthan Royals teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ahead of the Eliminator clash. Sinner's French Open pursuit hits a snag. It's got nothing to do with tennis Roland Garros: World Number One Jannik Sinner began his French Open campaign with a dominant straight-sets victory over Clément Tabur, extending his incredible winning streak to 30 consecutive matches. But his biggest challenge is not even on the clay court.

RCB used to rely on individual superstars, now they win as a unit IPL Qualifier 1, RCB vs GT: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's shift from a superstar-led model to a balanced team has come into focus through recent assessments by Ambati Rayudu and Tom Moody. The change reflects a wider structural overhaul in management and philosophy, with shared roles now driving results.

IPL 2026 Eliminator: Sooryavanshi's playoffs pressure test meets familiar SRH IPL 2026 Eliminator, SRH vs RR: Rajasthan Royals and Sunrisers Hyderabad meet in the IPL Eliminator with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi under the spotlight. Zee Entertainment in talks with FIFA over World Cup broadcast rights in IndiaIndia's Zee Entertainment Enterprises is in talks with FIFA to secure the streaming and broadcast rights for the 2026 FIFA World Cup in India, the company said on Tuesday, a move that could shake up the country's sports broadcasting landscape.

I feel like the Eiffel Tower: Naomi Osaka revels in dramatic French Open fashionJapan's former world number one Naomi Osaka said she is beginning to embrace her role as an entertainer on the WTA Tour after turning heads with a dazzling golden outfit during her first-round win at the French Open on Tuesday.

Rajat Patidar's 33-ball 93 anchored a record 254, before RCB's bowlers decimated Gujarat Titans with ease. In Dharamsala on Tuesday, Royal Challengers Bengaluru booked their place in a second consecutive IPL final, one win away from history.

Sriram Balaji, Yuki Bhambri make winning start to French Open in men's doublesIndia's Yuki Bhambri and N Sriram Balaji moved into the second round of the men's doubles event at the French Open 2026 after winning their respective opening-round matches in Paris on Tuesday.

French teenager becomes youngest male Grand Slam match-winner in 17 yearsWildcard teenager Moise Kouame stunned former champion Marin Cilic on his Grand Slam debut at Roland Garros. The straight-sets win underlined a rapid rise and delivered a rare age-record breakthrough.

Viral video takes cheeky dig at IPL's commercial overdriveIPL 2026: Popular X personality Ramesh Srivats takes a cheeky swipe at the relentless advertising creeping into modern cricket broadcasts, as multi-billion dollar team sales and massive media rights push IPL's commercial engine into a dizzying overdrive.

Bizarre: Sai Sudharsan throws his bat onto the stumps, out hit-wicket in Qualifier 1Gujarat Titans opener Sai Sudharsan suffered one of the most bizarre dismissals in IPL history during the Qualifier 1 clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Afghanistan announce squads for India tour, Rashid Khan skips one-off TestAfghanistan announced their squads for the upcoming tour of India on Tuesday, confirming that star spinner Rashid Khan will skip the one-off Test match but return for the ODI series beginning later in June.
